food_subgroup: Fresh herb leaf salad with dried shrimp and sesame-groundnut oil dressing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Pennywort Salad (Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke). Closest parent would be a mixed herb leaf salad concept. FoodOn does not currently enumerate Centella asiatica-based prepared salads.
ontology.foodex2_description: Fresh herb leaf salad (closest parent — FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Centella asiatica salad)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GTQ used as closest parent for fresh herb leaf salad. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Burmese pennywort salad.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this prepared dish.

preparation_method: dressed with lime juice and fish sauce

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Pennywort Salad is approximately 100g, typically presented as a starter plate at a Myanmar or Burmese restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Pennywort (Centella asiatica) — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table 2020 and USDA SR Legacy (FoodData Central)
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS and USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values for the prepared salad are derived by combining FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 data for Centella asiatica (raw herb leaf) with USDA SR Legacy values for dried shrimp, sesame seeds, groundnut oil, and fish sauce. The composite dish values represent a typical Burmese preparation at approximately 100g serving weight. Both FAO/INFOODS and USDA SR Legacy are approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Centella asiatica (pennywort) and mixed herb leaf salads — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for low-starch herb leaf vegetables and mixed vegetable salads
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke (Burmese pennywort salad) exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~20 is estimated by analogy with low-starch fresh herb leaf vegetables (GI 10–25 range per University of Sydney GI Database). The very low starch content of Centella asiatica leaves, combined with the fat and fibre from the dressing, supports a GI in the 15–25 range. GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (20 × 12) / 100 = 2.4. This is consistent with the FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 carbohydrate data for the herb leaf base.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 425mg per 100g serving (approximately 21% of the 2000mg daily limit), primarily from dried shrimp (~350mg) and fish sauce (~75mg)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit portion size to 100g or less and avoid adding extra fish sauce or dried shrimp; the pennywort leaves themselves are sodium-free and the potassium content (200mg/100g) provides a modest counterbalancing effect.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~20 (very low), GL ~2.4 per 100g serving; high dietary fibre (3.5g/100g) and fat dressing slow gastric emptying
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pennywort Salad is an excellent choice for patients with Type 2 Diabetes — the very low glycaemic load of 2.4 and high fibre content produce negligible postprandial glucose excursion, and consuming it as a starter before higher-carbohydrate courses may further attenuate the overall meal glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: MUFA-dominant fat profile from groundnut oil (estimated monounsaturated fat ~3.5g/100g), low saturated fat (1.2g/100g), zero trans fat, and sesame lignans with anti-inflammatory properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The MUFA-dominant fat profile and low saturated fat content make Pennywort Salad broadly suitable for cardiovascular disease patients; however, the 425mg sodium per serving warrants attention in patients with concurrent hypertension or heart failure, and portion control is advised.

warfarin_anticoagulation.key_factor: Centella asiatica (pennywort) is a very high Vitamin K source — estimated ~391mcg Vitamin K per 100g serving, approximately 5× the adult RDA of 75–90mcg
warfarin_anticoagulation.guidance: Patients on warfarin must be counselled that Pennywort Salad contains very high Vitamin K (~391mcg/100g); erratic consumption will destabilise INR — consistent intake at a fixed portion is strongly preferred over avoidance, and INR should be monitored when introducing or changing pennywort consumption frequency.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pennywort triterpenoids (asiaticoside, madecassoside), MUFA-dominant oil, sesame lignan sesamin, and Vitamin C (12mg/100g) provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant support
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pennywort Salad is generally suitable for rheumatoid arthritis patients due to its anti-inflammatory fat profile and bioactive triterpenoids; patients on disease-modifying antirheumatic drugs that interact with Vitamin K should note the high Vitamin K content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pennywort Salad (မြင်းကြာရွက်သုပ်, Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke) is a traditional Burmese fresh herb leaf salad made from raw Centella asiatica leaves — the same plant known as daun pegaga in Singapore and Malaysia, gotu kola in Sri Lanka, pegagan in Indonesia, and rau má in Vietnam. The salad is prepared by tossing fresh pennywort leaves with toasted sesame seeds, dried shrimp, fried garlic, fresh lime juice, fish sauce, chilli, and groundnut oil. The result is a bright, herbaceous salad with a slightly bitter, grassy flavour from the pennywort, a nutty richness from the sesame and groundnut oil, a savoury-umami depth from the dried shrimp and fish sauce, and a sharp citrus lift from the lime. Per 100g serving: approximately 145 kcal, 5g protein, 8g fat (MUFA-dominant from groundnut oil), 12g carbohydrate, 3.5g dietary fibre, 1.5g sugar, 425mg sodium. GI ~20 (very low), GL ~2.4 — one of the lowest glycaemic-load salads in the Southeast Asian food database. Clinically notable: Centella asiatica is an exceptionally high Vitamin K source (~391mcg/100g, approximately 5× adult RDA), which is critically relevant for patients on warfarin anticoagulation therapy — consistent rather than erratic intake is essential for INR stability. The salad is gluten-free and dairy-free. It is commonly served as a starter at Myanmar and Burmese restaurants in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. Source: FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table 2020 and USDA SR Legacy (FoodData Central).
common_user_queries: is pennywort salad good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 and USDA SR Legacy for the component ingredients (Centella asiatica raw leaf, dried shrimp, sesame, groundnut oil, fish sauce) and assembled as a composite dish estimate. No single approved source provides a direct analysis of the complete prepared Burmese pennywort salad dish. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for low-starch herb leaf vegetables. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. VITAMIN K CLINICAL ALERT: Centella asiatica (pennywort) is a very high Vitamin K source. Estimated ~391mcg Vitamin K per 100g serving (~5× adult RDA). This value is derived from FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 data for Centella asiatica raw leaf. Vitamin K is not included as a named field in the per_100g template but is documented here for clinical completeness. Warfarin patients require specific counselling on consistent pennywort intake for INR stability. 2. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite prepared salad dish is not directly available in FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 or USDA SR Legacy for this specific preparation. The raw Centella asiatica leaf moisture (~88% per FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020) is not directly applicable to the dressed salad after tossing with oil, dried shrimp, and lime juice; no published moisture measurement for the complete prepared dish was found in either FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 or USDA FoodData Central. This field is set to -1 due to limited published data for this traditional preparation. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (3.5g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.5g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy groundnut oil fatty acid profile applied to the ~8g total fat per 100g; these are not directly reported in FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 for the composite dish. 4. GI ~20 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Halal status is uncertain — fish sauce and dried shrimp are typically halal but sourcing and preparation standards vary; is_halal is set to false pending verified halal certification. 6. The input record ethnic_tags field contained 'Western' which appears to be a data entry error for a Myanmar dish; this has been corrected to Myanmar/Burmese in the output. 7. No core macro fields are zero; zero-macro justification rule (g4) is not triggered. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for groundnut oil and sesame; Centella asiatica leaves contain no trans fats.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is 'Pennywort Salad (Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke)' — precise canonical English name with Burmese romanized form. Local names include Malay (Ulam Pegaga Cara Myanmar) and Tamil (வல்லாரை சாலட்). Myanmar cuisine does not require Chinese, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese under G6 rules. Multilingual naming compliance satisfied for cuisine category.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal=145, protein_g=5.0, fat_g=8.0, carbohydrate_g=12.0 — all present, numeric, and non-zero. nutrient_source cites FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 and USDA SR Legacy, both approved primary sources under Project Veritas v2.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g=3.5, sodium_mg=425.0, saturated_fat_g=1.2, sugar_g=1.5 — all present as numeric values and plausible for a fresh herb leaf salad with dried shrimp and fish sauce dressing.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index=20, gi_category='low', glycemic_load_per_serving=2.4, gi_source present citing University of Sydney GI Database as analogous estimate basis. GI is not null and not zero. Estimation basis documented in gi_source.source_match_notes.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodEx2 code A0GTQ used as closest parent for fresh herb leaf salad — defensible and documented with gap note. FoodOn URI is null with honest gap note explaining no FoodOn URI exists for this specific dish. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented.

It explains what the food is, how it is prepared, taste and texture, cultural context (pan-Asian herb, Burmese restaurant starter), clinical relevance (Vitamin K / warfarin alert, GI/GL for diabetes), and key nutrients. common_user_queries and ingredient_synonyms are comprehensive.

Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes' as required. Additional conditions (warfarin, rheumatoid arthritis) included in additional_conditions.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Pennywort Salad (Myin Khwa Ywet Thoke) — G00010950. All six hard gates pass. Score 8/8. The record is a Myanmar/Burmese dish; G6 multilingual compliance does not require Chinese, Hindi, or other Singaporean-dish languages. Malay and Tamil local names are present as regional aliases given the pan-SEA distribution of Centella asiatica. Nutrient values are sourced from FAO/INFOODS SEA 2020 and USDA SR Legacy (both approved). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, documented transparently. Ontology uses FoodEx2 A0GTQ as closest parent with honest gap notes. All three required health conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) are present with specific clinical guidance. The critical Vitamin K / warfarin interaction is documented in both health_context additional_conditions and notes. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with full clinical and cultural context. Aliases self-check passed. water_g = -1 is fully documented with source names, reason, and confirmation of unavailability. Record is PASS.
